# showwhat

> Feature flags and config you own. Platform-agnostic.

showwhat is a definition format, rule engine, and visual tooling for feature flags and config. Define rules in YAML or JSON, evaluate them in your app, and store definitions wherever you want. Like OpenAPI for APIs, showwhat gives you a spec-first workflow with tooling built around it.

- Documentation: https://showwhat.yeojz.dev
- Repository: https://github.com/yeojz/showwhat
- License: MIT

## Monorepo Layout

```
packages/core/          @showwhat/core        — rule engine, schemas, parsers, evaluators
packages/showwhat/      showwhat              — main public API (re-exports core)
packages/configurator/  @showwhat/configurator — React component library for visual editing
packages/openfeature/   @showwhat/openfeature — OpenFeature Server SDK provider
apps/webapp/            @repo/webapp          — Vite + React SPA for managing definitions
apps/docs/              @repo/docs            — VitePress documentation site
internal/               distribution tests    — Node, Deno, Bun compatibility tests
```

## Package Dependency Graph

```
showwhat
  └── @showwhat/core (js-yaml, zod)

@showwhat/configurator
  ├── showwhat
  ├── @dnd-kit, radix-ui, lucide-react, clsx, tailwind-merge
  └── peer: react 19, react-dom 19

@showwhat/openfeature
  ├── showwhat
  └── peer: @openfeature/server-sdk ^1.0.0

@repo/webapp (internal)
  ├── @showwhat/configurator
  ├── showwhat
  └── zustand, react 19, tailwind v4

@repo/docs (internal)
  └── vitepress (embeds built webapp at /configurator/)
```

## Tech Stack

- TypeScript 5.4, ES2022 target, ESM only
- pnpm 10.30 workspaces, Turbo 2.8 for orchestration
- tsup for library builds, Vite 7 for apps, VitePress for docs
- React 19, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui primitives, Zustand 5
- Zod 4 for schema validation
- Vitest 4 with @testing-library/react for tests
- ESLint 10 + Prettier, lefthook for git hooks
- Runs on Node 22+, Deno, and Bun

## @showwhat/core

Low-level engine. Key exports:

- `resolve(definitions, context, evaluators)` — evaluate all definitions, return resolutions
- `resolveVariation(definition, context, evaluators)` — evaluate a single definition
- `builtinEvaluators` — condition types: env, string, number, bool, datetime, startAt, endAt, and, or
- `registerEvaluators(custom)` — merge custom evaluators with builtins
- `MemoryData` — in-memory data source implementing DefinitionReader and DefinitionWriter
- `parseYaml(str)`, `parseObject(obj)` — parse definitions from YAML strings or plain objects
- `parsePresetsYaml(str)`, `parsePresetsObject(obj)` — parse preset condition templates
- `createPresetConditions(presets, params)` — instantiate reusable condition templates
- `isWritable(reader)` — runtime check for DefinitionWriter support
- Zod schemas: Definition, Variation, Condition, Context, Resolution
- Typed errors: ShowwhatError, DefinitionNotFoundError, DefinitionInactiveError, VariationNotFoundError, ConditionError, ValidationError, ParseError, SchemaValidationError, InvalidContextError, DataError

Architecture: `schemas/` contains Zod types (no evaluator imports). `conditions/` contains evaluator implementations (imports from schemas). No default evaluators injected; consumers must pass evaluators explicitly.

## showwhat (main package)

High-level convenience API. Re-exports everything from @showwhat/core plus:

- `showwhat({ keys?, context, deps?, options: { data, evaluators?, fallback?, logger? } })` — resolve one or more keys, returns `Resolutions` record
  - `keys` provided: fetches each via `data.get(key)` in parallel
  - `keys` omitted: fetches all via `data.getAll()`
  - Per-key errors wrapped in `ResolutionError`, never thrown
  - Only systemic failures (context validation) are thrown
- `ResolutionError` — `{ success: false; key: string; error: ShowwhatError }` for failed keys
- `Resolution` — `{ success: true; key: string; value: unknown; meta: { variation, annotations } }`
- `Resolutions` — `Record<string, Resolution | ResolutionError>`
- Validates context against ContextSchema (Zod) automatically

Use `showwhat` for resolving flags with built-in evaluators and error wrapping. Use `@showwhat/core` directly for low-level batch resolution or custom evaluator injection.

## @showwhat/configurator

React component library for visually building definitions. Key exports:

- `Configurator` — main component, accepts a ConfiguratorStore
- `DefinitionEditor`, `DefinitionList` — definition CRUD
- `VariationCard`, `VariationList` — variation editing with drag-drop (@dnd-kit)
- `ConditionBuilder` — visual AND/OR condition nesting
- `ValueInput`, `DateTimeInput` — value editors
- `ConfiguratorStore` interface — apps provide their own persistence backend
- `useConfiguratorStore`, `useActionState`, `useConfiguratorSelector` — hooks
- `createPresetUI(presets)` — generate condition extensions from preset definitions
- CSS exported at `@showwhat/configurator/styles.css` (must be imported by consumers)

Design: controlled components (props in, callbacks out), no internal mutations. Store interface abstracts persistence so apps can back it with files, localStorage, or APIs.

## @showwhat/openfeature

OpenFeature Server SDK provider. Key exports:

- `ShowwhatProvider` — implements OpenFeature provider interface
- `ShowwhatProviderOptions` — configuration type for the provider
- `toShowwhatContext()` — maps OpenFeature evaluation context to showwhat context
- Supports boolean, string, number, object evaluation types
- Custom evaluators via options, lifecycle management, error code mapping

## Data Source Interfaces

- `DefinitionReader` — read interface: `get(key)`, `getAll()`, `listKeys()`, plus optional `load()`, `close()`, `ping()`
- `DefinitionWriter` — write interface: `put(key, def)`, `delete(key)`, `putMany(defs, opts?)`, plus optional lifecycle
- `DefinitionData` — combined: `DefinitionReader & DefinitionWriter`
- `MemoryData` implements both interfaces for in-memory use
- Custom data sources: implement `DefinitionReader` for read-only, add `DefinitionWriter` for read-write

## Definition Structure

A definition is a named rule-based configuration entry. It can represent a boolean flag, a config value, or a structured object:

```yaml
my-feature:
  description: Optional description
  active: true                    # set false to disable
  variations:
    - value: true
      conditions:                 # first match wins
        - type: env
          value: production
    - value: false                # catch-all (no conditions)
```

Variations are evaluated top-to-bottom. The first variation whose conditions all match wins. A variation with no conditions always matches (use as default).

Supported condition types: env, string, number, bool, datetime, startAt, endAt, and (nested), or (nested). Custom evaluators can be registered via `registerEvaluators()`.

## Custom Evaluators

- `registerEvaluators({ typeName: evaluatorFn })` — merge custom evaluators with builtins
- Each evaluator: `async ({ condition, context, annotations, deps, depth }) => boolean`
- `deps` — injected runtime utilities (hash functions, fetchers), passed as Readonly
- `annotations` — mutable record for attaching metadata to resolution results
- Fallback evaluator: `options.fallback` handles unknown condition types gracefully
- Reserved types `and` and `or` cannot be overridden

## Development Commands

```bash
pnpm install            # install dependencies
pnpm build              # build all packages via Turbo
pnpm dev                # watch mode for all
pnpm test               # build + run all tests with coverage
pnpm lint               # lint all packages
pnpm typecheck          # typecheck all packages
pnpm fix                # lint --fix + prettier
pnpm dev:docs           # docs dev server
pnpm dev:webapp         # webapp dev server
pnpm build:webapp       # build webapp + dependencies
pnpm build:docs         # build docs + dependencies
```

## Coding Conventions

- `interface` for contracts/extension points, `type` for data shapes and props
- No enums (use string unions), no `any` (use `unknown`)
- ESM only, no CommonJS
- Comments explain why, not what
- Test files: `*.test.ts` / `*.test.tsx`
- Entry points: `src/index.ts` with controlled exports
- Tailwind classes via `clsx()` and `tailwind-merge`
- Errors use typed hierarchy with context (key, message, type)

## Key Files for Understanding the Codebase

- `packages/core/src/index.ts` — core public API surface
- `packages/core/src/schemas/` — all Zod type definitions
- `packages/core/src/conditions/` — evaluator implementations
- `packages/core/src/resolver.ts` — resolution algorithm
- `packages/core/src/data.ts` — DefinitionReader, DefinitionWriter, MemoryData
- `packages/showwhat/src/index.ts` — main package entry and high-level API
- `packages/configurator/src/index.ts` — component library exports
- `packages/configurator/src/types.ts` — store interface contract
- `packages/openfeature/src/provider.ts` — OpenFeature provider implementation
- `apps/webapp/src/store/` — Zustand stores for webapp state
- `turbo.json` — build pipeline and task dependencies
- `pnpm-workspace.yaml` — workspace package locations
